2605.22737.title: The Distillation Game: Adaptive Attacks & Efficient Defenses
2605.22737.paper_summary: Distillation attacks create a deployment trade-off for model providers: the same outputs that make a model more useful can also make it easier to imitate. We study this trade-off through a minimax game between a utility-constrained teacher and an adaptive student. Our framework yields tractable one-sided response rules: an adaptive evaluation rule in which the student reweights high-value examples, and a teacher-side defense template that suppresses outputs most useful for distillation. From a cheap proxy for example value, we derive Product-of-Experts (PoE), a simple forward-pass-only defense that combines the teacher with a proxy student during generation. Empirically, adaptive evaluation reveals a large passive--adaptive gap: on state-of-the-art defenses, adaptive students recover substantially more capability than passive evaluation suggests on GSM8K and MATH. Under this stronger evaluation, the apparent robustness gap between expensive defenses and PoE narrows considerably, while PoE remains substantially cheaper and preserves higher-quality reasoning traces. Overall, our results suggest that strong distillation remains difficult to stop, and that progress on antidistillation should be judged against adaptive students rather than passive ones. Our code is available at: https://github.com/ysfalh/distillation-game.
2605.22737.paper_summary_zh: 蒸餾攻擊為模型提供者創造了一個部署權衡：使模型更有用的相同輸出也可能使其更容易被模仿。我們通過一個在效用受限的教師和一個自適應學生之間的最小最大博弈來研究這個權衡。我們的框架產生了可處理的單側反應規則：一種自適應評估規則，其中學生重新加權高價值範例，以及一個教師端防禦模板，抑制對蒸餾最有用的輸出。從一個便宜的範例價值代理中，我們推導出專家乘積（Product-of-Experts, PoE），這是一種簡單的僅前向傳遞的防禦，在生成過程中將教師與代理學生結合起來。實證結果顯示，自適應評估揭示了巨大的被動--自適應差距：在最先進的防禦下，自適應學生恢復的能力遠超過被動評估在GSM8K和MATH上所暗示的。在這種更強的評估下，昂貴防禦和PoE之間明顯的穩健性差距顯著縮小，而PoE仍然便宜得多，並保持更高質量的推理痕跡。總體而言，我們的結果表明，強蒸餾仍然難以阻止，對抗蒸餾的進展應該根據自適應學生而不是被動學生來評價。我們的代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/ysfalh/distillation-game。
2605.22737.author: Youssef Allouah et.al.
2605.22737.authors: Youssef Allouah, Mahdi Haghifam, Sanmi Koyejo, Reza Shokri
